0.title: ATAC-seq
0.content: Last updated: 12-19-2023 . ATAC-seq stands for Assay for Transposase-Accessible Chromatin with Sequencing. ATAC-seq is a widely adopted technique for profiling open chromatin regions throughout the genome. Recently, it has been extended to infer transcription factor occupancy by leveraging Tn5 insertion profiles, giving rise to a technique called digital ATAC-seq footprinting. By integrating these two types of information, ATAC-seq enables the identification of regions exhibiting active regulatory activity, such as enhancers and promoters, and facilitates the elucidation of the transcription factors involved in coordinating subsequent transcriptional processes. ATAC-seq method was first published in 2013 by Buenostro et al., Nature Methods at the Greenleaf Lab @ Stanford.

1.title: Role of Tn5 Transposase in ATAC-seq
1.content: Tn5 is a hyperactive transposase enzyme derived from the bacterium Tn5. It is used in ATAC-seq to efficiently fragment accessible DNA sites and incorporate sequencing adapaters. Tn5 also preferentially targets open chromatin regions because steric hindrance at non-accessible regions makes it difficult for adaptor incorporation.

2.title: How to process ATAC-seq data (Frazer Lab Pipeline)
2.content: . | Align (BWA and STAR produce very similar results) reference=/reference/private/STAR/hg19_gencode34 STAR --runThreadN 8 --alignIntronMax 1 --genomeDir $reference \ --genomeLoad NoSharedMemory --readFilesCommand zcat \ --readFilesIn ${dir}/R1.fastq.gz ${dir}/R2.fastq.gz \ --outSAMattributes All --outSAMunmapped Within \ --outSAMattrRGline ID:1 PL:ILLUMINA PU:CARDIPS LB:${id} SM:${id} \ --outFilterMultimapNmax 20 --outFilterMismatchNmax 999 --outFilterMismatchNoverLmax 0.04 \ --seedSearchStartLmax 20 --outFilterScoreMinOverLread 0.1 --outFilterMatchNminOverLread 0.1 \ --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted --outFileNamePrefix ${out_dir}/ . | Sort. Fixmate. Mark duplicates samtools sort -n -m 2G -@ 8 $bam -o ${dir}/${name}.nsorted.bam samtools fixmate -m -@ 8 ${dir}/${name}.nsorted.bam ${dir}/${name}.fixmate.bam samtools sort -m 2G -@ 8 -o ${dir}/${name}.sorted.bam ${dir}/${name}.fixmate.bam samtools markdup -@ 8 ${dir}/${name}.sorted.bam ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam samtools index -@ 8 ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam . | Optional: Get mapping stats (duplicates, chrM reads, etc.) samtools flagstat -@ 8 ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam > ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.flagstat samtools idxstats -@ 8 ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam > ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.idxstats samtools stats -@ 8 ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam > ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.stats . | Remove duplicates (-F 1024). Filter reads with mapping quality > 20 (-q 20). Keep reads in proper pair (-f 2). Keep fragments between 38-2000 bp. chrs=(`seq 1 22` X Y) chrs=chr`echo ${chrs[@]} | sed 's/ / chr/g'` samtools view -h -q 20 -f 2 -F 1024 ${out_dir}/${name}.mdup.bam $chrs |\ awk 'function abs(v) {return v < 0 ? -v : v}; substr($0,1,1)=="@" || (abs($9) >= 38 && abs($9) <= 2000)' |\ samtools view -b -@ 4 > ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam . | Index samtools index ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam . | Call narrow peaks macs2 callpeak -f BAMPE -g hs -t ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam -n ${name} --outdir ${out_dir} --nomodel --shift -100 --extsize 200 --call-summits . | Call broad peaks macs2 callpeak -f BAMPE -g hs -t ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam -n ${name} --outdir ${out_dir} --nomodel --shift -100 --extsize 200 --broad . | Count reads in promoters samtools view -c -L ${promoters_bed} ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam > ${out_dir}/reads_in_promoter.txt . | Convert peak file to saf (peaks2saf.pl). Count reads in each peak (featureCounts). cat $peak | peaks2saf.pl > ${saf} featureCounts -p -T 4 --donotsort -F SAF -a ${saf} -o ${out_file} ${out_dir}/${name}.filt.bam . | . Next steps: . | Remove peaks in blacklisted regions (from ENCODE) | TMM normalize read counts using edgeR | Generate consensus peaks by merging peaks across samples or selecting a subsample of high-quality samples and call peaks from their merged bam files. | .

3.title: Bun Rieu
3.content: Bun rieu, also known as tomato noodle soup . Ingredients: . | 4 chicken drumsticks | 6 cups of water | 2 lemongrass stalks (cut in half, peeled, and beatened) | 1 tbsp Instant Tom Yum sauce | 2 roma tomatoes | 1/8 tsp MSG | 3 tsp sugar | 3 tsp salt | Tofu cubes (as many as you want; fried or not, both are delicious) | . Steps: . | Boil water with chicken drumsticks, MSG, sugar, and salt | After water boils, add lemongrass. | Cover pot with lid and simmer for 12 min | Turn off heat. Place pot to the side and let sit for ~12 min. | Add tomatos, tofu, and tom yum sauce. Mix. | Serve with ~1/4 tbsp shrimp paste! | .

4.title: Che Chuoi
4.content: Che chuoi, also known as banana coconut pudding . Ingredients: . | 1 pound cubed taro | 0.5 pound cubed cassava (~$2.5/pound at grocery store, pre-peeled) | 1 packet vanilla sugar | 5 tbsp sugar | half of 1/3 cup peeled mung bean (yellow) | half of 1/3 cup dried tapioca pearl (pink and green rectangle) | half of 1/3 cup tapioca pearl | 5 cups water | 1 cup coconut cream | 0.5 tsp salt | . Steps: . | Wash taro and cassava. Cut into cubes. | Boil 4 cups of water. Cook cassava in boiling water for 8 minutes. Drain. | Wash dried tapioca pearl and mung bean. | Boil 5 cups of water. Add dried tapioca pearl and mung bean. Simmer for 10 minutes. | Add washed tapioca pearl and simmer for 15 more minutes | Add taro and simmer for 8 minutes | Add sugar, coconut cream, vanilla sugar, and salt | Serve hot or cold! Delicious at both temps! | .

5.title: What is Github?
5.content: GitHub is a web-based platform used for version control and collaboration on software projects. It provides developers with a centralized location to store, manage, and track changes to their code. GitHub utilizes the Git version control system, which allows multiple people to work on the same codebase simultaneously while keeping track of changes and facilitating collaboration.

6.title: Set up Git
6.content: . | Download . | Configure name and email for identification . | . git config --global user.name "Your name" git config --global user.email "your@email.com" . | Initialize repository git Initialize . Check by . ls -a . | Check status Check if there are untracked files or staged files that need to be added to repo git status . | Stage files git add filename1 filename2 . | Making commits git commit -m "Commit message" . | Check history of commit git log . | .

12.title: RNA-seq
12.content: Last updated: 12-19-2023 . RNA-seq, or RNA sequencing, is a technique used in molecular biology to analyze the transcriptome of a sample. The transcriptome refers to the complete set of RNA molecules, including mRNA (messenger RNA), rRNA (ribosomal RNA), tRNA (transfer RNA), and other non-coding RNA produced by the cells of an organism or a tissue. Most RNA-seq data measures mRNA levels, and specialized protocols are used for each type of RNA molecule being measured.

13.title: How to process RNA-seq data (Frazer Lab Pipeline)
13.content: . | Make scratch directory dir=`mktemp -d -p scratch` . | Set reference reference_star=/reference/private/STAR/hg38_gencode44/reference reference_rsem=/reference/private/RSEM/hg38_gencode44/reference/hg38_gencode44 . | STAR Alignment date >& 2 in_files=( ${dir}/R1.fastq ${dir}/R2.fastq ) cmd="STAR \ --runThreadN 12 \ --genomeDir ${reference_star} \ --genomeLoad NoSharedMemory \ --readFilesIn ${in_files[@]} \ --outSAMattributes All \ --outSAMunmapped Within \ --outSAMattrRGline ID:1 PL:ILLUMINA PU:CARDIPS LB:${name} SM:${name} \ --outFilterMultimapNmax 20 \ --outFilterMismatchNmax 999 \ --alignIntronMin 20 \ --alignIntronMax 1000000 \ --alignMatesGapMax 1000000 \ --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted \ --outFileNamePrefix ${dir}/ \ --quantMode TranscriptomeSAM" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Sort. Fill mate coordinates. Index date >& 2 cmd="samtools sort -m 2G -n -o ${dir}/Aligned.out.namesorted.bam -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.bam" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools fixmate -@ 12 -m ${dir}/Aligned.out.namesorted.bam ${dir}/Aligned.out.namesorted.fixmate.bam" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools sort -m 2G -o ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.bam -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.namesorted.fixmate.bam" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools index -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.bam ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.bam.bai" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Mark duplicates date >& 2 cmd="samtools markdup -@ 12 -s -T ${dir}/temp ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.bam ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools index -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam.bai" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Get mapping stats (total number reads, total mapped, total paired reads, total dupliciates, etc.) date >& 2 cmd="samtools flagstat -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam > ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam.flagstat" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools idxstats -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam > ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam.idxstats" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd date >& 2 cmd="samtools stats -@ 12 ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam > ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam.stats" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Run RSEM to generate read counts, TPM, and RPKM. Uses Transcriptome BAM file generated from STAR. date >& 2 cmd="rsem-calculate-expression \ --bam \ --num-threads 16 \ --no-bam-output \ --seed 3272015 \ --estimate-rspd \ --forward-prob 0" if [ ${#in_files[@]} -gt 1 ]; then cmd=$cmd" --paired-end" fi cmd=$cmd" ${dir}/Aligned.toTranscriptome.out.bam ${reference_rsem} ${dir}/rsem" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Reformat header for PICARD date >& 2 cmd="samtools view -H ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam | sed 's,^@RG.*,@RG\tID:None\tSM:None\tLB:None\tPL:Illumina,g' | samtools reheader - ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.bam > ${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.reheader.bam" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | Run PICARD to get more stats (pct_mrna_bases, pct_intergenic_bases, etc.) date >& 2 refFlat=/reference/public/UCSC/hg38/refFlat.txt.gz cmd="picard CollectRnaSeqMetrics \ I=${dir}/Aligned.out.sorted.mdup.reheader.bam \ O=${dir}/picard.RNA_Metrics \ VALIDATION_STRINGENCY=SILENT \ REF_FLAT=$refFlat \ STRAND=NONE" echo $cmd >& 2; eval $cmd . | .

14.title: LD Score Regression (LDSC)
14.content: . | Download baseline annotations | Download 1000 Genomes (European) LD scores | Download hapmap snps | Prepare GWAS input with columns SNP (rsid), POS, N, N_CASE, N_CONTROL, BETA, SE, MAF | . Tip: Make sure that MAF, N, N_CASE, and N_CONTROL have no non-numeric values and remove all empty values . | Prepare annotation files | Compute LD scores for your annotations (separate by chromosome) | Estimate heritability enrichment | .

16.title: Steamed Egg Cake
16.content: Ingredients: . | 1 pound ground meat (pre-seasoned with sugar, MSG, and pepper) | 5 whole eggs | 1 bunch of bean thread noodles | 1 tbsp sugar | 1/8 tsp MSG | Pepper (trust your gut) | 1 small onion | 2.5 tbsp fish sauce | 0.25 tbsp chopped thai chilli peppers | . Steps : . | Soak noodles in boiling water for 15 min | Mix all ingredients | Steam for 30 min | . Notes: . | Most popular bean thread noodles are the ones in a neon pink package | Use glassware to steam the egg cake | .

17.title: Build a website with Jekyll template
17.content: . | Create a GitHub repository | Clone repository to local git clone https://github.com/your-username/respository-name.git . | Install Ruby To check if you have Ruby installed, run ruby -v . If not, refer to the Ruby documentation for instructions (https://www.ruby-lang.org). | Install Bundler gem install bundler . | Set up Just the Docs template cd repository-name . Create a file named “Gemfile” and add the following lines . source "https://rubygems.org" gem "github-pages", group: :jekyll_plugins . Save and close “Gemfile”. Then, create a file named “_config.yml” and add the following lines . remote_theme: pmarsceill/just-the-docs title: My Docs description: Your site description . Save and close “_config.yml”. | Install webrick gem install webrick . If you have Ruby 3.0, you’ll need to add to Gemfile because it’s no longer provided. bundle add webrick . | Install the required dependencies bundle install . | Build and serve website bundle exec jekyll serve . Jekyll will start the local server and output a URL where you can view your website real-time. Open your web browser and visit the provided URL. | .
